Framing effect. This demonstrates that people respond differently to the same choice, depending upon how you frame the question. For example, the way a company uses statistics can influence your decision. They could say that their drug works on 90% of the patients who try it, but another company can state that this drug fails one in ten times. Advertising and marketing campaigns use this effect all the time. Next time you're watching TV, pay attention to how they present the numbers and use buzzwords. Please click on the options to learn a couple of the most important cognitive biases we run into.
